key_insight: AdsMind closes the loop between LLM-guided search and MLFF relaxation, using physics feedback to self-correct adsorption-configuration guesses and reliably find low-energy surface adsorbates.
engineering_relevance: This reduces the combinatorial search burden in heterogeneous catalysis, making adsorption discovery more practical for DFT-informed catalyst screening and autonomous workflow design.
method: Multi-agent LLM system proposes candidate adsorption configurations
MLFF relaxation feedback is used to detect and correct bad guesses in a closed loop
Backends are compared for robustness across benchmark surfaces and adsorbates
evidence_signal: Across AA20 and OCD-GMAE62, AdsMind achieved 100% and 98.8% success, with only 4.11 and 4.67 MLFF relaxations per case, and DFT checks on six AA20 systems confirmed correct adsorption-energy sign where open-loop outputs failed.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and transferability of the MLFF and benchmark coverage; stronger validation is still needed for more diverse surfaces, adsorbates, and edge-case chemistries.
paper_title: AdsMind: A Physics-Grounded Multi-Agent System for Self-Correcting Discovery of Adsorption Configurations on Heterogeneous Catalyst Surfaces
